name: Tandoori Chicken

hero.alt: Charred tandoori chicken on a cream ceramic plate with lemon wedges, onion rings, and mint chutney
hero.caption: tandoori chicken — charred, smoky, lemon-bright

healthFacts: Yogurt marinade contains lactic acid that physically denatures muscle fibers — making the meat measurably more tender.
Chicken breast has ~31g protein per 100g with only 3.6g fat — one of the leanest high-protein sources.
Kashmiri chili provides color via carotenoids without capsaicin heat — lycopene from a spice.
Fenugreek seeds in tandoori masala contain soluble fiber that slows glucose absorption.
High-heat grilling creates Maillard compounds that are the source of 'charred' flavor — not burning.
Naturally gluten-free — the marinade is yogurt and spices, no flour-based thickeners needed.

steps.title: Score the chicken deeply
steps.detail: Make deep cuts (1–1.5 inches) into the chicken pieces — 3 cuts per piece on the fleshiest parts. This is not optional: it lets the marinade penetrate to the bone and allows heat to reach the center faster.
steps.tip: No scoring = marinade flavor only on the surface. Deep scores = flavor all the way through.

media.alt: Raw bone-in chicken pieces with deep diagonal scores in a metal tray
media.caption: Deep scored cuts

steps.title: First marinade (acid layer)
steps.detail: Rub the chicken with lemon juice, salt, and Kashmiri chili. Mix in the cuts and on all surfaces. Let rest 15 minutes. This acid pre-treatment begins tenderizing and helps the second marinade adhere.
steps.tip: Two-layer marinating is how restaurants get that flavor depth. The acid layer is the key.

media.alt: Scored raw chicken rubbed with Kashmiri chili, salt, and lemon juice
media.caption: Chili-lemon first marinade

steps.title: Second marinade (yogurt layer)
steps.detail: Mix yogurt with ginger-garlic paste, tandoori masala, garam masala, cumin, oil, and a pinch more salt. Coat the chicken thickly — push marinade into all the cuts. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
steps.tip: Longer marination time is the single biggest upgrade you can make to this dish.

media.alt: Scored chicken pieces coated in thick orange yogurt tandoori marinade
media.caption: Thick yogurt-spice coat

steps.title: Bring to room temperature
steps.detail: Pull the chicken from the fridge 30–45 minutes before cooking. Cold chicken directly into a hot oven = uneven cooking — charred outside, undercooked inside. Room temp chicken cooks more evenly.
steps.tip: This rest step is especially important for thick, bone-in pieces.

media.alt: Marinated chicken resting on a wire rack over a foil-lined tray before broiling
media.caption: Room-temp rack rest

steps.title: High-heat cook
steps.detail: Place chicken on a wire rack over a foil-lined tray. Broil at maximum heat (230–250°C) for 20–25 minutes, turning once at 12 minutes. Baste with melted butter or ghee when you flip. You want charring on the edges — that's the point.
steps.tip: The char is flavor, not burnt. Aim for dark spots on the edges while the flesh stays juicy.

media.alt: Charred tandoori chicken cooking on a wire rack with ghee and a basting brush
media.caption: High-heat char and baste

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.detail: Pull when the juices run clear or internal temp hits 74°C. Rest on the rack for 5 minutes — the carry-over heat finishes the job. Sprinkle chaat masala, squeeze lemon, serve with mint chutney and sliced onion rings.
steps.tip: Chaat masala on top is what makes tandoori chicken taste like a restaurant — don't skip it.

media.alt: Rested tandoori chicken served with lemon wedges, onion rings, and mint chutney
media.caption: Rest, dust, and serve

utensils.name: Oven with broil setting (or outdoor grill)
Wire rack over a baking tray
